## How  Canada monitors respiratory viruses
FluWatch+  is Canada's national surveillance system that monitors the spread of  respiratory viruses, such as:
- other respiratory viruses
The  FluWatch+ surveillance system has an interactive dashboard. This technical dashboard  provides a weekly overview of key trends on the activity of respiratory  viruses.
View  interactive dashboard

### Overview of surveillance system
A  more detailed description of how the national FluWatch+ surveillance system  works to track respiratory viruses is available.
Technical overview of respiratory virus  monitoring in Canada

## Subscribe for updates
If  you’d like to be notified when new respiratory virus reports are published each  week, become a subscriber.
Subscribe for updates
Help  us track respiratory viruses in your community.
Sign up to be a FluWatcher
Some  FluWatch+ data comes from volunteers across Canada who report weekly on  symptoms such as cough and fever. They're known as FluWatchers. This  information helps public health officials identify where respiratory viruses  are circulating.
The  weekly questionnaire has 2 questions about symptoms and takes about 15 seconds  to complete. Your answers are anonymous.
- FluWatch+ weeks calendar
- Weekly and annual respiratory virus reports
- Access provincial, territorial and international data
